reforms.description: The 1981 Economic Recovery Tax Act (ERTA) introduced significant tax cuts and an Accelerated Cost Recovery System (ACRS) for capital allowances, aimed at stimulating business investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing tax burdens and incentivizing capital investment, the reform is expected to enhance business investment and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was passed and implemented in 1981, leading to tax reductions.
reforms.importance_rationale: The ERTA represented a major overhaul of the tax system with significant implications for business investment and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The 1981 Economic Recovery Tax Act (ERTA) made across-the-board cuts in income tax rates and introduced an Accelerated Cost Recovery System (ACRS) for capital allowances.
